prose:
Method–provenance summary
• Property: atom-resolved Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰), dual descriptor, and most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for aniline; headline figure reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 2.2975867 on atom 3 (C, index 2 in 0-based counting).
• Level of theory: DFT tier standard → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (RIJK) in PySCF.
• Geometry provenance: single-point charges on the supplied fixed geometry “/home/erobles/…/aniline.xyz”; no geometry optimisation was performed (DFT//input-xyz).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 for the neutral, +1 / 2 for the (N+1) cation, −1 / 2 for the (N−1) anion that define f⁺ and f⁻.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend PySCF) using Mulliken partitioning.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged for all three charge states; integrity block says trustworthy: true. All engine warnings are reproduced verbatim below.

Engine warnings (verbatim)
– sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
– sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
– common header present

Reproducibility
CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgctdctw_/live.json
Live log streamed to fukui_*.out in the current directory; result JSON at …/live.json; figure at …/aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
